M2 _ L4 . 	Webinar Registration
Page Errors to Avoid

MODULE 2 / LESSON 4: WEBINAR REGISTRATION PAGE ERRORS TO
AVOID
ERRORS TO AVOID ON YOUR WEBINAR
REGISTRATION PAGE
#1. The page is not designed for mobile. Test your webinar registration page
on mobile… Not just looking at it, but actually signing up. The page needs to
load quickly, everything needs to be easy to read, and the sign up button
needs to be on the first page.
#2. The sign up button is below the fold. If your sign up button isn’t yet
above the fold, it needs to be. You may need to shorten the copy in order to
give the sign up button enough space.
#3. The sign up button disappears into the design of the page. Your sign
up button needs to stand out. Use a bold color and design, and make sure the
button color isn’t used anywhere else on the page.
#4. The page is too complex. An overly complex page can hurt your
registration. Simplify your page, remove elements or cut copy, and use colors
and images to draw people through to the sign up button.
#5. Not telling them this is a free webinar. Both “free” and “webinar” need
to be clearly communicated, ideally in the pre-head (the small type above the
headline).
#6. Too many choices. Keep it to 3 webinar time options.
#7. Too many form fields to complete. Your sign up process needs to be
quick and easy. You just need name and email (optional is the mobile # (to
receive a text message).
#8. Not running analytics. Put analytics code on the page. This is especially
important if the page isn’t on your own site (for example, if you’re using
Leadpages or Clickfunnels). This way, you’re able to track what’s happening
on your page.
#9. Not including tracking pixels. Your page needs to include tracking
pixels. At minimum, you need the Facebook pixel and Google Analytics.
Ideally, you’re also firing Google Tag Manager events across your entire
funnel.

-- 2 of 3 --

MODULE 2 / LESSON 4: WEBINAR REGISTRATION PAGE ERRORS TO
AVOID
ADDITIONAL ERRORS TO AVOID
These are some additional errors to avoid on your webinar registration page:
Adding testimonials to your registration page. Don’t add testimonials to
your registration page unless you’ve done a test and they are proven to be
helping conversions.
Adding an About-You section. Don’t include an About-You section unless
you have something very specific to this webinar in your background that
should be brought up.
